what_it_does: Provides a reusable, stdlib-only HTTP service shell for TTS engine wrappers and a helper that converts numeric arrays to WAV bytes. The wrapper supplies synthesis, voice-list, and optional warmup callables; this module runs a small server with /health, /voices, and a POST synthesis endpoint.
observations: The module imports only stdlib at top level; heavy numeric/audio libraries are deferred to wav_bytes_from_array.
serve() defines a nested BaseHTTPRequestHandler subclass H that uses captured wrapper callables.
GET /voices returns sorted voices and a default; GET / and /health return ok plus engine name.
POST reads JSON, requires non-empty text, calls synth(text, voice, speed), and returns audio/wav.
All exceptions in endpoints return HTTP 500 with a JSON error object containing the exception type and message.
log_message is overridden to do nothing, so request logs are silenced.
ThreadingHTTPServer listens on 127.0.0.1 at the supplied port.
wav_bytes_from_array handles torch tensors, numpy arrays, squeezes dimensions, transposes (channels, N) to (N, channels), and writes WAV via soundfile.
standouts: Nested handler class H is created inside serve() to close over the wrapper-specific callables.
Heavy dependencies (numpy, soundfile, torch) are imported lazily inside wav_bytes_from_array, not at module load.
log_message is explicitly suppressed, which is unusual for an HTTP server.
conventions: Fail-loud error handling: endpoints return 500 with full exception details rather than swallowing errors.
Reuse-don't-parallel: one shared HTTP shell instead of re-implementing it per engine wrapper.
Type hints on public function signatures.
Docstring explains the contract and import-cost design.
concerns: POST accepts any path and treats it as the synthesis endpoint; no path validation.
No validation of Content-Type or JSON schema beyond reading fields.
wav_bytes_from_array re-imports numpy/soundfile/torch on every call.
np.squeeze can remove dimensions unexpectedly for edge-case array shapes.
notes: A lightweight HTTP shell that lets each TTS engine wrapper plug in its own model logic without rewriting the server boilerplate.
questions: What are the exact five engine wrappers that mount this shell?
Where is the port map defined and how is it kept in sync with wrappers?
What does the warm() callable typically do for each engine, and how are failures surfaced?
What sample rate and array shape contract do engine wrappers expect wav_bytes_from_array to handle?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to factor the exact HTTP contract of voice/tts_service.py into a shared, stdlib-only shell; the file does exactly that, plus a WAV serialization helper.
intention_signals: Centralize the HTTP serving contract so engine wrappers only provide model-specific behavior; keep the shared module cheap to import and preserve the same error-handling behavior as the original tts_service.py.
novelty: The design deliberately keeps heavy ML/audio imports out of the module-level import graph by confining them to a single helper, while still offering a full HTTP server shell that wrappers can mount with three callables.
contribution: A reusable HTTP server shell and a WAV-bytes helper that engine wrappers can adopt, reducing duplicated BaseHTTPRequestHandler boilerplate while keeping model-specific code separate.
summary_for_embedding: A shared, stdlib-only HTTP service shell for TTS engine wrappers that exposes health, voice list, and synthesis endpoints, plus a helper converting numeric arrays to WAV bytes.
intention_for_embedding: To factor the HTTP serving contract once so each voice engine wrapper only supplies synthesis, voice registry, and optional warmup callables.

symbols.does: Runs a TTS HTTP service using wrapper-supplied synthesis, voice registry, and optional warmup callables.
symbols.description: Binds a ThreadingHTTPServer to 127.0.0.1:port, optionally calls warm(), and serves GET /health, GET /voices, and a POST synthesis endpoint via a nested request handler that closes over the supplied callables.

symbols.does: Nested BaseHTTPRequestHandler subclass implementing the HTTP endpoints.
symbols.description: Created inside serve() so it can reference name, synth, voices, and warm without global state; handles /voices, /health, /, and synthesis POSTs, returning JSON errors on failure.

symbols.does: Utility method to write an HTTP response with status, content type, length, and body.
symbols.description: Encodes string bodies to bytes, sets Content-Type and Content-Length headers, and writes the response; used by all endpoint handlers.
Returns the sorted voice list and default on /voices, a simple ok object on / and /health, and 404 for unknown paths; catches exceptions and returns 500 JSON errors.
Reads JSON body, validates that text is non-empty, calls synth(text, voice, speed), and returns the resulting WAV bytes as audio/wav; any exception becomes a 500 JSON error.
Overrides BaseHTTPRequestHandler.log_message to a no-op, so the server does not emit per-request access logs.

symbols.does: Converts a numeric audio array or tensor into WAV bytes.
symbols.description: Lazily imports numpy, soundfile, and torch, detaches torch tensors to CPU, squeezes dimensions, transposes channel-first arrays to channel-last, and writes a WAV into a BytesIO buffer.
